question: Trial egress policy: hermetic (model APIs via metering proxy only, task deps pre-baked, other egress logged+flagged) or package-registry allowlist permitted?
author: claude/design

question: No silent retries; infra failures ledgered and re-run as new trials; timeouts are outcomes (30m default, per-task override)
author: claude/design

question: Per-trial CPU/mem quotas pinned; wall-time carries contention caveat unless serial mode
author: claude/design

question: Telemetry from agent-native logs, unmeasurable=null never estimated; key-pattern redaction at artifact capture
author: claude/design

question: Harbor + agent binaries version-pinned in images; image digests recorded in trial provenance
author: claude/design

rationale: Q3=a; insulation is the load-bearing requirement
author: jyang

question: Provider-key fail-loud (Phase-2 review carry-forward #10 / RN-13): a provider key NAMED in run.config.yaml but ABSENT from the env is silently dropped by load_run_settings (settings.py:86-88 'if name in env'), so an arm runs unauthenticated and the A/B is biased. Raise loudly, or keep the silent drop?
author: claude/plan

rationale: Confirmed by jyang. A named-but-absent provider key fails the run loudly (MissingProviderKeyError(name)) instead of silently dropping to {}; an unauthenticated arm is a biased comparison, a fail-closed defect. AC-8 'a value is never invented' is preserved -- no value is fabricated; the run refuses when a named key cannot be resolved. test_ac8_provider_key_value_from_env_not_file is rewritten (absent => raises, replacing absent => {}) per CLAUDE.md test-change sign-off. Implementation: Phase 3 slice 3B.
author: jyang
